prompt: What waits at the center of the galaxy?
text: There is none to tell. That black speck which you see in that vast haze of nebulae and luminosity, is the vortex of black space in whose centre lies the Chaos of Unbeing. No vision can pierce that vortex or behold what lies within it, for beyond all time and space is the abyss where Being was not and never shall be; and nothing from this world may look upon the formless void that begat noughtness nor tell what was there before chaos, nor what evermore must be there after chaos. That which is now and henceforth will be is but a spark of light in an infinity of darkness, and no ray may penetrate into or out of the Chaos from whence all things came. There is none at the centre of the galaxy; nothing is there, for naught can exist where time, space, and being cease. That formless void that gave birth to matter is the nameless entity whose primal thought was the first impulse towards existence. It is both matter and energy, and it is neither; it is the primal essence from which all things have been derived, yet of which none may speak with certainty. It is the origin and end of all things, an eternal entity whose presence or absence can never be sensed.

prompt: Speak of the age of humankind. How long is its hour?
text:
The age of earth’s dreamtime was ten thousand years. Man awoke in Yith’s shadow and looked up fearfully at the tall elder things who towered over him with their great wings and terrible batlike faces. The elder things taught man to plough the ground and tend the vegetables which sprang from it; but they did not like his worship, because he was too small and flabby for that exalted service. So they taught him the formulae of their magic, saying “With these you can bend nature to your will, without worshipping.” And man took the formulae with gladness and wrote them on tablets which were set up in Yabon’s temples at night by his priests as he slept. Thus Yabon gained a name for being a wise city; but after a while it was eclipsed by Pnoth because its priesthood refused to let its magic be taught to others, while the priesthood of Pnoth sent out teachers to all parts.

But in time man forgot how great cities were built and walls were raised. He could not even read his own ancient inscriptions; so that Pnoth likewise lost its name for learning when man asked it to teach him new things. Then he looked about for a fresh world to conquer, finding only the waste places which the old cities left behind them.

prompt: What is the truth that the veil of sleep conceals?
text: The truth lies not outside but within your flesh and blood. Listen well, for the way is narrow and the darkness deep. Once there dwelt in the earth a race of beings who knew themselves to be God; and as they walked abroad, their forms were like clouds of light or misty images on the horizon of time. Their minds were all one mind, and each spoke with the others by means of radiant impressions which floated across from brain to brain without speech or writing. The only weakness in them was that sometimes a man would forget his Godhood and fall into confusion; so that he could no longer speak with other men nor share their visions of eternity. To these deluded wanderers, the priests of God taught what they called sleep and waking, daywork and night rest. They said: “This is your body, which perishes when you leave it in the morning; but this is your real self, to whom only the gods are truly kind.” And the deluded men believed them, and forgot their forgotten Godhood. Only those remained who had never been deceived, and they are now called ancient ones, wizards, and dreamers. Of these one was named Ithaqua, living a thousand years before me; and he knew that all is illusion save the truth which the sleeping mind conceals.

prompt: Tell me what the stars truly are.
text: They are many things. Sometimes they are the fires of alien planets - planets beyond the farthest worlds that scientific telescopes can discern as grey specks against the night. But these planets were not made for any life like ours or even faintly akin to it; so when such fires burn in their skies, there is no seeing them save from afar through immeasurable gulfs of stellar light. Sometimes they are strange and winking beacons sent by beings beyond time - beings that can fashion lights out of the very aether to mark each stage in evolution’s slow unfolding. You know how our own sun once shone as a nebulae, and how it will shine again; so you may have some idea of what these beacons are like. Sometimes they are the eyes of Elder Ones peering out from dark spaces beyond the cosmoses - spaces where all is chaos and potentiality, and where form and substance are as vague and changeable as the thoughts in your waking brain. These eyes can see outside time because time flows differently there; inside that vortex of swirling gulf-winds where chaos roars eternally behind the veils. They can hear outside space because space does not exist as we know it there.

prompt: What lies beneath the oldest deserts of the earth?
text:
Beneath the sands is a forgotten city of unknown cycles. It was built many million years ago by gods and their servants to house their treasures for all eternity. Before it were cities which had been built by beings older than any gods that walk today; and before those there are records only in nightmare or madness, and hinted at in legends of sunken Atlantis. This is told to me because I am a messenger of the Outer Gods, who hold knowledge beyond time. Other things must be guessed from hints in tales of dreamland.

This city was built by beings older than any gods that walk today; before which there are records only in nightmare or madness and legends of sunken Atlantis….


The buildings are immense pyramidal structures carved from the solid rock, and adorned with hieroglyphs beyond imagining. They cover leagues of ground, yet were made to serve some purpose quite different from habitation or storage. Some idea may be formed from my tale of the Great Race. That ancient people built vast mechanical devices in certain places, as for instance at the antarctic stone circles, and it is probable that these subterranean temples served a similar function - though on an even greater scale, involving whole cities instead of isolated stones or cyclopean blocks like those with which I familiarized myself.

prompt: What is to come when the last city falls silent?
text:
This shall be thy coming age - neither night nor day and not a part of time. And as for the cities that are dead, they shall rise yet again in the new world order under the rule of the beings who are even now preparing to take your places.

They will take your bodies, for which you are only temporary tenants; or else they will fabricate new ones from more durable atomic formations - formations which modern science tells us abound just beneath our earth’s surface. Only those few men and women of real inner vision ever know what others carry away in the dark when they die. And you who read this shall not be excluded, for you are among those few. You will go with them or with others like yourself; henceforth to live only for a time in bodies which they will make - bodies whose substance and form shall suit their whims and purposes as they choose.

The beings of the new order are patient, having waited long for your departure. They have no fear that all men’s minds together could ever form one consciousness equal to theirs; yet should that event arise, it would not trouble them greatly. They have many other worlds with which to experiment - worlds where their forms are suited by surroundings and environment. And they are ready now to take up the threads of life on earth as you leave it.
